name: Apple App Store Connect API
what_it_does: Apple's REST API for App Store Connect — enabling iOS/macOS/tvOS/watchOS app developers to automate app management, manage certificates and provisioning profiles, upload builds, manage app metadata, read analytics data, manage subscriptions and in-app purchases, and run automated TestFlight workflows without manual App Store Connect portal interaction.
use_cases: Automating App Store build uploads via App Store Connect API for CI/CD pipelines
Managing TestFlight beta testing via App Store Connect API for automated beta distribution
Fetching sales and analytics data via App Store Connect API for revenue dashboards
Managing provisioning profiles and certificates via App Store Connect API for team automation
Updating app metadata and screenshots via App Store Connect API for localization workflows
not_for: Non-Apple platform apps (Android/Windows — use Google Play API for Android)
End-user app functionality (App Store Connect API is for developer tooling, not in-app features)
Real-time user analytics beyond Apple's aggregated data
best_when: An iOS/macOS development team needs to automate App Store workflows — CI/CD integration, beta testing distribution, metadata management, and analytics reporting without manual portal access.
avoid_when: You don't publish Apple platform apps or need end-user-facing app functionality (different APIs).

auth.notes: JWT (JSON Web Token) authentication. Private key (.p8 file) + Key ID + Issuer ID from App Store Connect portal. JWT signed with ES256 (ECDSA with P-256 curve). Token expiry: maximum 20 minutes. Authorization: Bearer {jwt_token}. No OAuth2. No refresh tokens — generate new JWT per session. Private key downloadable once (keep secure). Apple Developer Program membership required.

pricing.notes: Apple Inc. (Cupertino, CA). API launched 2018. OpenAPI specification available for code generation. JWT-based auth using ES256 elliptic curve. Private key (.p8): generated in App Store Connect, downloadable ONCE — if lost, must revoke and create new key. Tools: Fastlane (popular CI/CD tool), altool (deprecated), notarytool, xcrun agvtool. Rate limiting: 100 requests/second/key. App resources: apps, builds, beta groups, devices, certificates, profiles. Analytics: sales, subscriptions, crash rates. App metadata: descriptions, screenshots, review information. Localization management. Subscription management (auto-renewable, non-renewable).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JWT ES256 (20-minute expiry). Private key (.p8) downloaded once. 100 req/sec. OpenAPI spec available. Fastlane integration. Apple Developer Program required ($99-299/year). No refresh tokens.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: App reads idempotent by app ID. Build reads idempotent by build ID. Metadata updates idempotent. Beta group assignments idempotent. Build submission not idempotent — check status first.
known_agent_gotchas: Private key (.p8) downloaded ONCE — store securely immediately (cannot re-download, must revoke and recreate)
JWT maximum expiry: 20 minutes — implement token refresh before each API session
ES256 JWT signing: use ECDSA P-256 private key, not RSA (different from many JWT implementations)
OpenAPI spec available: download from Apple developer docs for client generation
Rate limit: 100 req/sec per API key — per-key limit, not per-account
Fastlane tools (spaceship, deliver, pilot, match) wrap this API — consider using Fastlane for CI/CD

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. ES256 JWT (strong signing). 20-minute token expiry limits exposure window. Private key (.p8) downloadable once — high-value secret. Apple security posture (SOC2, enterprise grade). App Store data (revenue, users, builds) is highly sensitive business data. No refresh tokens — forced re-authentication every 20 minutes. Apple Developer Program membership verification required.
